commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi L100 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi L100's 4G63 petrol and 4D68 diesel engines dominate UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this decision tool to compare repair vs replacement costs.

problems.group: Mitsubishi L100 4G63 Timing Chain Tensioner Failure
problems.h4: Mitsubishi L100 4G63 Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why Petrol Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi L100 2.0 Van, Pickup, Panel Van (1996-2007, 4G63)
problems.rootCause: Original tensioner (MB062044) loses hydraulic pressure, allowing chain slack that damages guides and sprockets.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, chain, guides before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; prevents engine damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with upgraded tensioner, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for engines over 120k miles or with damage

problems.recommendation: On a Mitsubishi L100 worth £3,000-£6,000, preventative tensioner replacement makes sense if caught before rattling starts. Once noise appears, replacement is usually more economical than repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G63 engine prices for your Mitsubishi L100

problems.h4: Mitsubishi L100 4D68 Injector Failure - Why Diesel Engines Cut Out
problems.rootCause: Common rail injectors wear internally, causing poor atomisation, rough idle and eventual no-start conditions.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four injectors, code to ECU, test rail pressure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine otherwise healthy; may not address rail wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt diesel long block with upgraded injectors, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage commercial vehicles

problems.recommendation: For fleet L100s over 150k miles, a rebuilt 4D68 with upgraded injectors offers better long-term reliability than piecemeal repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 4D68 Mitsubishi L100 diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Mitsubishi L100 Engine Overheating - Head Gasket Failure Risk
problems.rootCause: Radiator (MB806087) or water pump (MD622553) failure leads to overheating, warping cylinder head and blowing head gasket.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, machine surface, fit upgraded gasket kit (MD640840)
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block undamaged; risk of hidden warpage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with upgraded cooling components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Safest option if overheating was severe or prolonged

problems.recommendation: If your L100 overheated and now shows white smoke or oil/coolant mixing, replacement is usually more reliable than repair.
problems.cta: Compare Mitsubishi L100 rebuilt engine prices for overheating damage

problems.h4: Mitsubishi L100 4G63 Oil Consumption - Valve Seal & Ring Wear
problems.rootCause: Valve stem seals harden with age; piston rings wear, allowing oil into combustion chamber causing blue smoke.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace valve seals via cam cover removal; does not address ring wear
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if consumption is mild; rings may still leak

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with upgraded seals and rings, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for engines consuming over 1L per 1,000 miles

problems.recommendation: If your L100 is burning more than 1L oil per 1,000 miles, replacement is more economical than repeated top-ups and repairs.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt 4G63 engines for high-mileage Mitsubishi L100

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi L100
finalCta.paragraph: The Mitsubishi L100 is a workhorse van where downtime costs money. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi L100 specialists for rebuilt engines that address known failure points. Every unit includes upgraded components and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, with nationwide delivery or supply & fit available. Compare quotes and get your commercial vehicle back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi L100 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi L100 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

Panel van and pickup body styles available

keyChanges: H11A chassis launched with 4G63 2.0L petrol engine
4D68 2.3L common rail diesel added 1998 for fuel economy

faq.h2: Mitsubishi L100 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi L100 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Mitsubishi L100 engines typically cost £2,200-£3,100 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. The common 4G63 petrol ranges £2,400-£2,800, while the 4D68 diesel sits £2,200-£3,100.
keyPoints: 4G63 petrol rebuilt: £2,400-£2,800 supply-only
4D68 diesel rebuilt: £2,200-£3,100 supply-only
Supply & fit adds £600-£1,200 depending on location
All rebuilt units include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi L100 engine replacement prices now

items.question: Is the Mitsubishi L100 4G63 engine reliable?
items.answer: The 4G63 is generally reliable but has a known timing chain tensioner weakness around 120,000 miles that drives most replacement enquiries for UK L100 vans.
keyPoints: Timing chain tensioner (MB062044) fails at ~120k miles
Rebuilt units include upgraded tensioner to prevent repeat failure
Head gasket risk if cooling system neglected
With maintenance, 4G63 can reach 150-200k miles
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 4G63 Mitsubishi L100 engines

items.question: What causes Mitsubishi L100 diesel engines to fail?
items.answer: The 4D68 diesel's main failure point is injector wear after 150,000 miles, causing rough idle, cutting out and eventual no-start conditions on commercial L100s.
keyPoints: Common rail injectors wear internally at high mileage
Symptoms: rough idle, loss of power, hard starting
Rebuilt engines include upgraded injectors and rail components
Fuel economy makes 4D68 popular despite injector sensitivity
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi L100 4D68 diesel engine prices

items.question: Should I repair or replace my Mitsubishi L100 engine?
items.answer: For L100s over 120k miles with major failures, replacement is usually more economical than repair, especially for commercial vehicles where downtime costs money.
keyPoints: Repairs viable only for minor, early-stage issues
Rebuilt engines address all wear points in one job
12-month warranty protects against repeat downtime
Supply & fit options minimise vehicle off-road time
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi L100 engine supply & fit deals

items.question: Which Mitsubishi L100 engine is best to buy used?
items.answer: For used L100 purchases, the 4G63 2.0L petrol offers the best balance of availability, parts support and rebuild affordability in the UK commercial market.
keyPoints: 4G63 has strongest UK aftermarket support
Rebuilt units readily available at £2,400-£2,800
Check service history for timing chain maintenance
Avoid units with overheating history or oil/coolant mixing
items.cta: Compare used Mitsubishi L100 engine options

items.question: How long does a rebuilt Mitsubishi L100 engine last?
items.answer: A properly rebuilt Mitsubishi L100 engine with upgraded components can reliably deliver another 80,000-120,000 miles with regular maintenance and commercial use.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines replace all wear items: bearings, seals, gaskets
Upgraded timing components address known failure points
Follow service intervals for oil and cooling system
12-month unlimited mileage warranty provides peace of mind
items.cta: Get quotes for long-life rebuilt Mitsubishi L100 engines

items.question: Can I fit a different engine to my Mitsubishi L100?
items.answer: Engine swaps are possible but complex; sticking with the original 4G63 or 4D68 code ensures compatibility with ECU, wiring and chassis mounts on UK L100s.
keyPoints: 4G63 and 4D68 share mounting points across H11A/H12A
ECU and wiring harness must match engine code
Chassis code (H10A/H11A/H12A) affects compatibility
Specialist advice recommended before attempting swaps
items.cta: Enquire about Mitsubishi L100 engine compatibility
